title: Australia's Economy Slowing Sharply With Jobs Set to Be Lost -- Market Talk
full_text: Other Editions 29 April 2026 Australia's Economy Slowing Sharply With Jobs Set to Be Lost -- Market Talk Dow Jones Newswires 28 April 2026 00:39 GMT 141 words English DJDN Copyright © 2026, Dow Jones & Company, Inc. 0039 GMT - Australia's near-term outlook increasingly features higher inflation coupled with slower GDP growth. National Australia Bank chief economist Sally Auld continues to see growth slowing this year to around 1.5% before a modest pickup over 2027. The slowing in growth sees unemployment drift up to a peak of around 4.75% by late 2027, she adds. Cumulatively since the outbreak of the war in the Middle East, NAB has also revised up its expectation for underlying inflation by 0.5 percentage points, and now sees underlying inflation peaking at 3.9% in 2Q 2026 and headline inflation peaking at 4.9%, she adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; X @JamesGlynnWSJ) (END) Dow Jones Newswires April 27, 2026 20:39 ET (00:39 GMT) Dow Jones & Company, Inc. summary: Australia's economy is projected to slow significantly, with GDP growth expected to decrease to around 1.5% in 2026. Unemployment is anticipated to rise to approximately 4.75% by late 2027, while underlying inflation is forecasted to peak at 3.9% in the second quarter of 2026. These economic changes may lead to job losses and increased financial pressures on households.
publication:
Australia lacks national strategy to regulate AI spread in workplace, report states
Australian Broadcasting Corporation News28 April 20261,245 wordsnational work reporter Bronwyn Herb

Australia risks repeating the mistakes it made with social media unless it moves quickly to regulate the spread of artificial intelligence in the workplace, according to a new report.
key_points: Unemployment expected to peak at 4.75% by late 2027
Underlying inflation forecasted to reach 3.9% in Q2 2026
relevance: Understanding these economic trends is crucial for actuaries and financial services professionals in assessing risks and making informed decisions.
